units.name: Deadeye
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

units.name: Phantom
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

units.name: Grid
units.prompt: Trap the paths enemies must take to reach your backline. Stay at medium range. Use pulse on clustered enemies. Denial blocks adjacent threats.

units.name: Aegis
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

units.name: Ghost
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

units.name: Bolt
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

actions.thinking: Round 1, perfect time to cloak. All enemies are far away (5-7 tiles), no threats adjacent. I'll cloak first, then advance toward Bolt (the enemy striker/damage dealer at 1,5) — moving to (1,3) gets me 3 tiles closer while staying on the same column for a direct flank approach next turn.

Round 1 — cloak immediately per orders and TIP. Deadeye is the priority target at (2,1). I'm at (3,4), enemies are 4 tiles away. Cloak first, then move toward Deadeye to close distance. Moving to (2,2) gets me closer while staying cloaked and out of range.

Both enemies are at distance 5, out of range. Need to advance to get within range 2 for precision_shot. Moving to (2,3) gets me closer while keeping distance from threats. No enemies in range this turn.

Deadeye is at dist=3, just outside my range-2 precision_shot. I need to close to range ≤2 without going adjacent (which blocks precision_shot). Moving to (1,3) puts me at dist=2 from Deadeye at (2,3) — exactly in range. No movement penalty if I move first then shoot... wait, precision_shot does 1dmg if I moved. Still worth it for 1dmg. Alternatively, move to (2,4) which is dist=1 from Deadeye — that's adjacent-ish but (2,3) to (2,4) is dist=1, not adjacent diagonally... dist=1 means adjacent, blocking precision_shot. Best: move to (1,3), dist=2 from Deadeye, not adjacent, precision_shot fires at 1dmg (moved penalty). Or stay and wait — but Deadeye is out of range at dist=3. I must move. Move to (1,3) → precision_shot Deadeye for 1dmg.

Round 1, no enemies in range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Moving to (3,3) brings me closer to Deadeye at (2,3) — that's adjacent range next turn. I'll then shield_wall facing South to block incoming fire from the enemy cluster below, protecting myself and nearby allies.

Round 1, all enemies at distance. Aegis is at (3,3) heading south toward us, Bolt at (1,5). I should advance to mid-range and set a trap on a key approach tile. Moving to (3,2) puts me at distance 1 from Aegis — too close for trap (blocked by adjacent enemy). Move to (3,1) keeps distance 2 from Aegis at (3,3), and I can place a trap at (3,2) which is directly in Aegis's path. That way when Aegis advances south, they'll step on the mine.

I'm cloaked with 1 turn remaining, behind Deadeye (striker) at (2,3) — prime target as the enemy damage dealer. I'm adjacent and behind them, perfect for shadow_strike which keeps cloak. Grid's denial zone blocks breach/cloak but NOT shadow_strike. I should shadow_strike Deadeye now to deal damage while staying cloaked, then move away from Grid's denial zone to preserve options next turn.

I'm cloaked with 1 turn remaining. Ghost (enemy specter) is adjacent at (1,2) facing W, and I can reach position (2,2) which is behind Ghost. Moving to (2,2) puts me adjacent and behind Ghost, then I can use shadow_strike to deal damage while maintaining cloak. This also prioritizes killing enemy damage dealers. Bolt is the priority striker but unreachable this turn. Ghost is the immediate threat.

Aegis is adjacent at (3,3) - precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to a Vector, but Aegis is a sentinel, not a Vector. Wait, precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector. Grid is our ally Vector. Aegis is adjacent (dist=1) but is a sentinel, so precision_shot should work against Aegis. However, range is ≤2 tiles and Aegis is at dist=1, so it's in range. But I need to check: precision_shot requires LoS and is blocked 'when adjacent to enemy Vector' - no enemy Vector adjacent, Grid is allied Vector. So I can use precision_shot on Aegis for 2dmg. Ghost at (1,2) is dist=2, also in range. Aegis is healthy (5HP sentinel likely), Ghost is healthy specter. Orders say prioritize killing wounded - none wounded. Use precision_shot on closest threat Aegis to chunk it down. No need to move first since Aegis is already in range and I haven't moved (full 2dmg).

Deadeye is at distance 2 from my current position (1,5). I'm not adjacent to any enemy, so precision_shot is not blocked. I haven't moved yet, so I can fire for full 2 damage. Fire precision_shot at Deadeye first, then wait or hold position.

Grid is adjacent at (3,2) and has DENIAL active blocking my abilities — only basic attack works. I can attack Grid directly since it's in range. Orders say push forward and attack when possible. Staying put keeps FORTIFY active, but I should attack Grid first then consider position. Attack Grid (in range, dist=1), then use shield_wall facing S toward the enemy cluster (Deadeye at (2,4), Phantom at (2,2)). Wait — DENIAL blocks abilities, so shield_wall won't work. Best play: attack Grid to damage/disrupt, then wait to keep FORTIFY.

Aegis is adjacent at (3,3) - my Denial passive is already blocking their abilities. Ghost is at range 2 to my west. I can't use trap while adjacent to Aegis. Best play: pulse hits Aegis for 1 damage since they're within 1 tile. Then move to (3,4) to maintain distance from Ghost and keep Aegis in range while setting up trap next turn.

Phantom is adjacent at (2,2) facing S — its behind tile is (2,3). I can move to (2,3) to get behind Phantom, then use breach to take control of it. Phantom is healthy but breach would neutralize it for a turn and let me use it against Deadeye. Alternatively, I can shadow_strike Phantom now since it's in range. But breach is the stronger play — move to (2,3) to get behind Phantom, then breach it. This removes the specter threat and potentially turns it against Deadeye.

secondAction.addendum: Move toward Deadeye at (2,4) and attack.

actions.thinking: Orders are to move toward Deadeye at (2,4) and attack. But Deadeye is an ally — this must mean I'm breached and under enemy control. Ghost is at (2,3) between me and (2,4). I need to reach (2,4) to attack Deadeye. I'm currently at (2,2). Ghost is at (2,3) — my Ghost Step passive lets me move through enemies. I can move through (2,3) to reach (2,4), then attack Deadeye.
actions.firstResult: Specter can move through but not end on occupied tile

actions.thinking: Ghost is adjacent at (2,3) - precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to enemy. Bolt is at (1,5) dist=2, not adjacent, so I can use precision_shot on Bolt. But I'm at 2/5 HP and Ghost is right next to me threatening. I should move away from Ghost first to enable precision_shot. Moving to (4,4) gets me away from Ghost and keeps range on enemies. From (4,4), Bolt at (1,5) would be dist=4, out of range. Aegis at (3,3) would be dist=2, in range. Let me move to (3,5) - dist from Ghost becomes 2, Aegis at (3,3) is dist=2. Or stay and use suppressing_fire on Ghost to slow them. Ghost is at dist=1 (adjacent) - suppressing_fire range is ≤2 tiles so it works. Slowing Ghost reduces their threat. Then I can still act. Actually: fire suppressing_fire at Ghost first (slows them), then move away. Suppressing_fire targets a tile and hits a 2-tile line from target - need to pick direction carefully. Target Ghost at (2,3), the line goes 2 tiles from target. This slows Ghost. Then move to (4,4) to escape.

Grid(vector) is 3 tiles away at (3,4) - currently in range from (1,5). I can use precision_shot on Grid but it's healthy and not priority. Deadeye and Phantom are wounded but out of range at dist 4. I need to close distance to reach them. Moving to (2,4) gets me closer to Phantom at (2,2) - dist would be 2, bringing it in range. But Grid is at (3,4), and (2,4) would be 1 tile from Grid - adjacent! That blocks precision_shot. Moving to (2,5) keeps me 2 tiles from Grid and gets me closer. From (2,5), Phantom at (2,2) would be dist 3 - still out of range. Let me check (1,3): dist to Grid(3,4) = sqrt(4+1)=~2.2, not adjacent. From (1,3), Phantom at (2,2) = dist ~1.4, so 1 tile - in range for precision_shot! And Grid is 2+ tiles away so no blocking. Move to (1,3) then precision_shot Phantom.

Aegis is adjacent at (3,3) - my Denial passive is already suppressing their abilities. Ghost is wounded at (2,3) within range 2. Bolt is healthy at (1,3) out of range. Phantom is critical at (2,2) and needs protection. I should pulse now to hit Aegis and potentially Ghost (both within 1 tile of me at (3,4): Aegis at dist=1 yes, Ghost at dist=2 no - pulse is 1 tile AoE so only hits units within 1 tile). Pulse hits Aegis for 1dmg. Then I can place a trap on a key chokepoint to protect Phantom's position. Tile (2,3) where Ghost is standing - can't trap occupied tile. Tile (3,2) is valid move tile but also a path between enemies and Phantom. Actually I should trap (2,2) or nearby to protect Phantom, but Phantom is there. Trap on (3,2) to block Aegis/Ghost advance toward backline. But wait - trap is blocked when adjacent to enemy, and Aegis is adjacent! I cannot use trap. So: pulse Aegis (hits adjacent units within 1 tile - Aegis at dist=1 qualifies), then move to (4,3) to maintain pressure while repositioning away from Bolt's approach.

Grid is adjacent at (4,3) and in denial zone blocking my abilities except basic attack. I'm fortified (50% dmg reduction). Orders say push forward and attack when possible. Grid is healthy but adjacent - I should attack Grid to remove the denial zone threat. I can attack Grid (dist=1, adjacent) as basic attack still works in denial zone. After attacking, I could move but staying fortified is valuable at 6/9 HP. Attack Grid first, then wait to keep fortify bonus.

Phantom is at (2,2) facing S, I'm at (2,3) which is directly behind them — perfect for shadow_strike to keep cloak, or breach if cooldown were ready. Breach cooldown is 1 turn remaining so can't use it. Phantom is critical HP, shadow_strike will likely finish them. I'm suppressed so only 1 tile of movement. Shadow_strike keeps cloak and hits adjacent — use it on Phantom to eliminate them. No movement needed since they're already adjacent.

I'm at 1HP - critically low. Ghost is wounded and at dist=3, just out of my range-2 precision_shot. Aegis is healthy at dist=2, in range. Priority: I need to either finish Ghost (wounded) or survive. Ghost is at (2,3), dist=3 - out of range. If I move to (3,4), Ghost becomes dist=2 and in range for precision_shot. But moving means precision_shot does only 1dmg - Ghost is wounded so might survive. Aegis is adjacent-ish at dist=2. I should move to (3,4) to get Ghost in range, then use precision_shot on Ghost. Moving reduces shot to 1dmg but Ghost is wounded - likely low HP, may finish them. Alternatively stay and use precision_shot on Aegis (dist=2, healthy) for 2dmg. Orders say prioritize killing wounded enemies. Move to (3,4) to reach Ghost, fire precision_shot for 1dmg (moved penalty) - risky. Better: stay at (4,4), shoot Aegis for 2dmg since Ghost is out of range anyway. But orders say prioritize wounded. I can't reach Ghost without moving. Move to (3,4): Ghost dist becomes 2, shoot for 1dmg. At 1HP I'm very fragile. Let me move to (5,4) to retreat slightly, but that doesn't help reach Ghost. Best play: move to (3,4), precision_shot Ghost - 1dmg on wounded likely kills.

Deadeye is critical and in range at dist=3. I'm at (1,3) and Deadeye is at (3,4) - that's exactly 2 tiles away diagonally but dist=3 by manhattan. Precision_shot has range ≤2 tiles. Dist to Deadeye is 3, so out of precision_shot range from current position. Need to move closer. Moving to (2,4) puts me closer to Deadeye at (3,4) - dist would be 2, within precision_shot range. Grid is at (4,3), moving to (2,4) keeps me 2 tiles from Grid (not adjacent, so no denial). From (2,4) I can precision_shot Deadeye at (3,4) for 2dmg to finish them off.

Grid is adjacent at (4,3) to my east. I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Ghost is critical to my west at (2,3). I should attack Grid first while fortified, then shield_wall facing east to protect Ghost from Grid. DENIAL zone blocks complex abilities but basic attack should work.

Aegis is adjacent at (3,3) - Denial is active blocking their abilities. Ghost is critical at (2,3) range 2. Both are in range. Pulse hits all within 1 tile - Aegis at dist 1 would take damage. But Ghost is at dist 2, out of pulse range. Better to attack Ghost to finish them off (critical = low HP), then move away or use pulse on Aegis. Actually, attack range is adjacent only (dist 1), so I can only attack Aegis. Ghost at dist 2 is within my rng2 for trap. I can't trap while adjacent to enemy (Aegis blocks trap). I could move first to break adjacency with Aegis, then trap Ghost's path. Move to (4,4) to get distance from Aegis, then pulse or trap. From (4,4): Aegis at (3,3) = dist 2, Ghost at (2,3) = dist 3. Pulse from (4,4) won't hit anyone. Better: stay, use pulse to hit Aegis (dist 1), then move away. Pulse hits all within 1 tile = Aegis takes 1 dmg. Then move to (5,3) to reposition.

I'm at 1HP so I need to be careful. Grid is the only enemy, 3 tiles away. I can move to (4,3) which is directly behind Grid (Grid faces E, so behind is west side at x=4). From (4,3) I'll be adjacent and behind — perfect for breach. Move to (4,3) then use breach on Grid.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from allies and toward the edge of the map

actions.secondResult: Cannot use breach — adjacent to enemy Vector (Denial)

actions.thinking: Grid is at distance 4, out of my range-2. Need to close to range 2. Moving to (4,4) gets me to distance sqrt((5-4)^2+(3-4)^2)=sqrt(2)≈1.4, adjacent - that would block precision_shot. Moving to (3,4) puts me at dist=sqrt(4+1)≈2.2, still out of range. Moving to (4,4) would be adjacent. Best option: move to (3,4) which is dist ~2.2 from Grid - still out of range but closer. Actually let me check (3,4): dist to (5,3) = sqrt((5-3)^2+(3-4)^2)=sqrt(4+1)=2.24, still out of range-2. What about (4,4)? dist to (5,3)=sqrt(1+1)=1.41 - that's adjacent range, blocks precision_shot and puts me in denial zone. I should advance to best available position without entering adjacency. (3,4) is the closest I can get while staying >2 tiles. Wait this turn or move closer - orders say position for sight lines. Move to (3,4) to be ready for next turn shot if Grid moves closer.

At 5/9 HP with fortify active, I need to balance aggression with survival. Grid is at (5,3), 2 tiles away — moving to (4,3) would be adjacent but Ghost is already there. Moving to (4,4) gets me closer while avoiding blocking Ghost. However, orders say push forward. I can move to (4,3) isn't available (Ghost is there). Best aggressive move is (4,4) to close distance, then shield_wall facing E to protect against Grid's attack. This maintains fortify if I don't move... but orders say push forward. I'll move to (4,4) to advance and set up shield_wall East to protect Ghost and myself from Grid.

Ghost is critical and adjacent at dist=1 - pulse will hit Ghost (dist=1) and possibly Aegis (dist=2, outside pulse's 1-tile AoE). Ghost is critical so pulse should finish or severely damage them. Aegis is wounded at dist=2. I'm at (5,3) facing east. Pulse hits all within 1 tile - that's Ghost at (4,3) for sure. After pulse, move to (5,4) to reposition and set up denial/trap coverage. Actually, better to move first to (5,4) to potentially bring Aegis into pulse range too, but Aegis is at (4,4) which would be dist=√2≈1.4 from (5,4) - still outside 1-tile range. Stay put and pulse to eliminate Ghost, then move away.

Grid is exactly 2 tiles away at (5,4) - within precision_shot range and not adjacent, so no blocking. I'm at full HP (5/5). I can fire precision_shot for 2 damage without moving, preserving full damage. Grid is healthy so this won't kill but will wound it. Stay put and shoot.

Aegis is adjacent at (4,4) - my Denial passive is already blocking their abilities. I'm wounded at 4HP. Bolt is at dist=2. Pulse would hit Aegis for 1dmg (they're wounded, this could be impactful) and potentially Bolt if they're within 1 tile of me - but Bolt is at (3,4) dist=2, outside pulse range. Best play: pulse Aegis now for chip damage, then move north to (5,5) to create distance and avoid being sandwiched. Trap is blocked since I'm adjacent to Aegis.

Grid is at (5,5), dist=2. I can move to (5,4) to get adjacent and attack. Moving will lose Fortify but orders say push forward aggressively. From (5,4) Grid at (5,5) is adjacent — attack. No ally shield_wall needed right now.

Grid is wounded at (5,5), dist=3, within precision_shot range (≤2 tiles means dist≤2... wait, range:≤2 tiles means dist≤2). Grid is at dist=3, so currently OUT of precision_shot range. Need to move closer but must avoid going adjacent (dist=1). Moving to (4,5) would put me at dist=1 from (5,5) — too close, triggers denial. Moving to (4,4) puts me at dist from (5,5): |4-5|+|4-5|=2, that's 2 tiles away, safe and within precision_shot range. Fire precision_shot at Grid from (4,4) for 2dmg since I moved (1dmg if moved). Grid is wounded so 1dmg might finish or weaken further.

I'm at 2HP — critically low. Aegis is adjacent at (5,4), wounded. Bolt is at (4,4), healthy. My Denial passive is already blocking Aegis's abilities since they're adjacent. I need to finish off Aegis who is wounded. Pulse hits all units within 1 tile — that would hit Aegis at dist=1 and potentially hurt me too, but I'm already critical. Actually pulse deals 1dmg to ALL units within 1 tile including me, so I'd die. Better to use attack on Aegis (1dmg, adjacent) to try to finish them. Aegis is a sentinel with likely 8-9 HP but is 'wounded' — unclear if 1 attack kills. Moving away first: I can move to (3,5) or (4,5) to break adjacency with Aegis, then trap a path. If I move to (4,5), I'm still dist=1 from Aegis and dist=1 from Bolt — bad. Move to (3,5) puts me dist=2 from Aegis and dist=2 from Bolt — better, and I can trap from there. But trap is blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector... I'm Vector, and the warning says trap is blocked when adjacent to an enemy Vector. Aegis is not a Vector, so trap should be fine. Place trap at (4,4) or (5,4) to catch Bolt or finish Aegis. Actually, move to (3,5) first to escape, then trap at (4,4) where Bolt currently stands — but Bolt might move. Better to trap (4,5) as a chokepoint they'd cross. With 2HP I need distance.

I'm fortified at (5,4) with 50% damage reduction. Grid is at (3,5) distance 3, not in attack range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Moving to (4,5) gets me closer to Grid while staying adjacent to Bolt at (4,4). From (4,5) I can use shield_wall facing W to protect Bolt from Grid's attacks. Moving will lose fortify but the aggressive push is ordered.
